design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate building levels as Gizmo, defeat gremlins using varied weapons, reach Gremlin Control Center
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Weapon switching and enemy avoidance while progressing through obstacle-heavy level design
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Gizmo defending against gremlins using creative tools (tomatoes, matchstick bow, flashlight)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: IP-driven level design tied to movie locations; emphasis on varied weapon types as primary progression hook rather than character growth

difficulty.lives_system: Standard NES lives system with multiple attempts per level
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system for progression saves between sessions (regional variation: Japanese version uses separate password screen)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional balancing indicates developer iterative difficulty tuning—Japanese version features heart replenishment per level and reduced spike hazards, suggesting accessibility adjustments for market

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon arsenal expansion—progression unlocks new tools (genetically modified super tomato, matchstick-firing bow, flashlight, frisbee) as players advance through building levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level-based gatekeeping; weapons tied to specific environment contexts (laboratory tomatoes, later-stage matchsticks)
replayability_hooks: Password system enables save-scumming and alternative route experimentation
Varied weapon types encourage different tactical approaches per level
Regional differences (JP version easier layout) suggest speedrun/challenge variation potential

world_building_techniques: Building locations tied to movie set pieces (Clamp Cable Network building, laboratory, Gremlin Control Center)
Contextual weapon placement (laboratory environment → tomato weapons)
Enemy type distribution reflects movie gremlin variants

notable_bosses: Mohawk gremlin (appears in Stage 2-3 with hitbox/positioning differences across regions)

danger_signals: Spike traps and pit hazards (reduced in Japanese version, indicating regional difficulty perception)
Frag mine enemies with enlarged hitboxes (Japanese version)
environmental_storytelling: Gremlin Control Center as final objective—centralizes narrative goal
Building progression from public spaces to restricted laboratory/control areas
Weapon contextualization in environmental spaces (tomatoes in lab, matchsticks in later areas)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Gremlins 2: The New Batch (NES)
Wikipedia - Gremlins 2: The New Batch (video game)
